rulings.Case Title: CREDIGY RECEIVABLES, INC. VS. FENG ZHU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order To Quash To Set Aside Default, And Vacate Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 24, 2008, line 2, DEFENDANT FENG ZHU Motion For Order To Quash To Set Aside Default, And Vacate Default Judgment. MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT IS DENIED. THE MOTION MUST BE BROUGHT WITHIN TWO YEARS UNDER CCP SECTION 473.5. ROGERS V. SILVERMAN (1989) 216 CAL.APP.3D 1114, 1121-1122. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: GREG MORRIS VS. SAFECO INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings On The 3rd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 24, 2008, line 3, DEFENDANT OPTION ONE MORTGAGE CORPORATION, PREMIER TRUST DEED SERVICES Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On The Third Amended Complaint. The motion is sustained without leave to amend, there being no opposition, to the following causes of action: 16th bad faith, 17th constructive trust, 19th fair practices, and 20th debt collection practices. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the following causes of action: 4th (conspiracy) for this party is not a state actor, 8th (intentional infliction of emotional distress), 9th (privacy), and 14th (misrepresentation) for leave to amend was not granted as to these causes of action. The motion is sustained as to the following causes of action and leave to amend will be granted only if plaintiff demonstrates an ability to do so: 10th for fraud where plaintiff must plead the requisite elements as to this defendant; 11th where plaintiff must plead facts demonstrating that there is a basis to conclude that the foreclosure sale was not regularly and fairly conducted (see 6 Angels Inc. v. Stuart-Wright Mortgage Inc. (2001) 85 Cal.App.4th 1279-1284); 17th for conversion to plead the contractual basis for the claim and a fixed sum of money capable of being converted; and the 21st to plead facts that support a ficuciary duty claim. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GREG MORRIS VS. SAFECO INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 24, 2008, line 3, DEFENDANT CITY OF FRESNO, FRESNO FIRE DEPARTMENT DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the following causes of action: 9th for violation of privacy, 13th for obstruction of justice and the 14th for misrepresentation for failure to file a timely government claim. Defendant McAlpine is not a peace officer and Government Code section 945.3 is not applicable. The demurrer is sustained as to the following causes of action: the 4th for conspiracy, the 5th for due process and the 6th for constitutional violations. The complaint alleges that plaintiff sought mental health treatment in October 2003. By that time over two years had passed since plaintiff was the subject of a criminal complaint. Under CCP section 335.1, the applicable two year statute of limitations had run. Owens v. Okurae (1989) 488 U.S. 235 . Leave to amend will not be granted unless plaintiff can plead facts that demonstrate that the claim period was tolled. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GREG MORRIS VS. SAFECO INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 24, 2008, line 3, DEFENDANT CITY OF FRESNO, FRESNO FIRE DEPARTMENT MOTION TO STRIKE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. The moving parties' request for judicial notice is granted. The motion to strike August 15, 2002 from the Third Amended Complaint and substitute August 15, 2001 is granted, no opposition having been filed. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GREG MORRIS VS. SAFECO INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 24, 2008, line 3, DEFENDANT FRESNO DISTRICT ATTORNEY'S OFFICE, ELIZABETH EGAN DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained as to all claims against the District Attorneys' Office. It is a subunit of the county and not a person within the meaning of section 1983. United States v. Kama (9th Cir. 2005) 394 F.3d 1236, 1239 The demurrer is sustained as to Elizabeth Egan. Leave to amend will be granted only if plaintiff is able to articulate actionable conduct on the part of this defendant. Plaintiff must demonstrate that the conduct is not protected under the immunity given to prosecutors to initiate and prosecute a case as contrasted with the investigation of a crime. Imbler v. Pachtman (1976) 424 U.S. 409, 431; Broan v. Bogan (9th Cir. 2003) 320 F.3d 1023, 1029-1030; and Gov't Code 821.6. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GREG MORRIS VS. SAFECO INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 24, 2008, line 3, DEFENDANT CRAWFORD AND COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the following causes of action: 8th intentional infliction of emotional distress, 9th right to privacy and 14th misrepresentation because these causes of action were added without seeking leave of Court. The demurrer to the 4th cause of action for conspiracy and the 16th cause of action for negligence is sustained without leave to amend. Crawford owed no duty of care to plaintiff. Sanchez v. Lindsey Morden Claim Services, Inc. (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 249, 253-255. Cooperating with law enforcement is not actionable conduct. Arnold v. IBM (9th Cir. 1981) 637 F.2d 1350. The demurrer is sustained as to the 10th cause of action for fraud. Leave to amend will be granted only if plaintiff is capable of establishing all of the required elements - the falsity, who did it, when was it done, what was done and to whom was the misrepresentation made. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GREG MORRIS VS. SAFECO INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 24, 2008, line 3, DEFENDANT GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to following causes of action: 4th conspiracy, 8th intentional infliciton of emotional distress, 9th privacy, 14th misrepresentation, 15th claims act, 16 negligent investigation and 20 constructive trust. Leave to amend was not granted as to the 4th, 8th, 9th and 14th causes of action. No opposition was asserted as to the 15th cause of action. There is no duty of care applicable to the carrier and so the negligence claim fails. See Croskey & Heeseman, CAL.PRAC. GUIDE: INSURANCE LITIGATION (The Rutter Group) section 11.205, p.11-48. A constructive trust may not be imposed on funds that defendant admittedly does not possess. The demurrer is sustained as to the 10th cause of action for fraud for failure to plead the requisite elements; sustained as to the 12th cause of action for bad faith for failure to plead the terms of the policy; and sustained as to the 19th cause of action for conversion. Leave to amend will be granted only if plaintiff is able to demonstrate that fraud may be plead with particularity, that plaintiff is capable of pleading the terms of the policy at issue and can plead a sum of money capable of being converted and a factual basis that the sum was converted. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CAL-CON PUMPING, LLC VS. WESTERN TRUCK PARTS & EQUIPMENT COMPANY, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 24, 2008, line 5, DEFENDANT WESTERN TRUCK PARTS & EQUIPMENT COMPANY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. PLAINTIFF MAY PLEAD THE MEANING OF AN ALLEGED CONTRACT WHERE THE INTERPRETATION IS REASONABLE. ARAGON-HAAS V. FAMILY SECURITY INS. SERVICES, INC. (1991) 231 CAL.APP.3D 232, 239. PLAINTIFF HAS DONE SO IN PARAGRAPHS 14 AND 29. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CAL-CON PUMPING, LLC VS. WESTERN TRUCK PARTS & EQUIPMENT COMPANY, LLC et al
